title: Add Travis CI file for cross platform building
body:
Includes a mandatory Linux job and an optional MacOS one as at this
moment we do not have support for that platform.

comments.body:
Hi @ssbarnea. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

We don't want Linux testing on Travis - their kernels are rather ancient.
We have Cirrus to cover Ubuntu tests now. I'm fine with an optional OS X
job, though.

On Thu, Nov 22, 2018, 08:21 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com wrote:

> bot, test pull request
> @cevich <https://github.com/cevich> PTAL
>
> —
> You are receiving this because your review was requested.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1854#issuecomment-441029136>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCAckYRSvi3zWcfPi70sch5dci2tBks5uxqTRgaJpZM4YvQRY>
> .
>

So OsX can "technically" be done by Cirrus-CI native, with [something called *Anka/MacStadium*.](https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/supported-computing-services/#anka) Making that happen would require registering for their services (easy) and figuring out how to pay for it (hard).  The cost looks like it would far exceed our monthly budget for ***all*** the testing we do in GCE...by a factor of 4 :cry: 

A simpler way though, may be to figure out how to do a cross-compile on another OS.  Then [call a script after integration tests run]( https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/integration_test.sh#L17).  That's [basically the last step of the `full_vm_testing` task](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/.cirrus.yml#L64), so the script would be free to make whatever breaking changes are needed to do a cross-compile.  Not a perfect solution, but it could work.

As far as convincing ubuntu (or any other OS) to do the cross-compile, that's not something I would know how to do.
@cevich @ssbarnea @mheon What should we do with this PR?

IMHO, Matt's statement is the easy-way:

> I'm fine with an optional OS X job, though.

I'm not sure if it's possible to have a single Travis job which is optional, maybe it can work?.  Even so, if the job always reports success, will anyone notice when there's a problem?
we no longer can have optional tests while spread out over multiple CI frameworks I'm afraid.
Well I think "optional" in this context means "Always passes".  That's why I question, when it silently fails who will notice and do something?  (it's the "noticing" part I'm worried about).
Since we have had no futher feedback Closing.  Reopen if this is stil something you wish to pursue.
